# UC-15.1.3 · Power Redundancy Status

## Description

Loss of A/B feed redundancy means a single power failure will cause an outage. Immediate awareness enables emergency response.

## Value

Loss of A/B feed redundancy means a single power failure will cause an outage. Immediate awareness enables emergency response.

## Implementation

Monitor PDU input status and UPS input voltage. Alert immediately on loss of any power feed. Track ATS (Automatic Transfer Switch) events. Maintain power topology documentation for impact analysis.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: SNMP TA, PDU/UPS events.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: PDU input status, UPS input voltage, transfer switch events.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Monitor PDU input status and UPS input voltage. Alert immediately on loss of any power feed. Track ATS (Automatic Transfer Switch) events. Maintain power topology documentation for impact analysis.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=power sourcetype="snmp:pdu"
| where input_status!="normal" OR input_voltage < 180
| table _time, pdu_name, rack_id, feed, input_status, input_voltage
```

Understanding this SPL

**Power Redundancy Status** — Loss of A/B feed redundancy means a single power failure will cause an outage. Immediate awareness enables emergency response.

Documented **Data sources**: PDU input status, UPS input voltage, transfer switch events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SNMP TA, PDU/UPS events. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: power; **sourcetype**: snmp:pdu. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=power, sourcetype="snmp:pdu". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where input_status!="normal" OR input_voltage < 180` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Power Redundancy Status**): table _time, pdu_name, rack_id, feed, input_status, input_voltage


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PDU UI or SNMP poll for the same devices, readings, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (rack × A/B feed status), Table (power events), Timeline (redundancy loss events), Single value (racks with full redundancy %).

## SPL

```spl
index=power sourcetype="snmp:pdu"
| where input_status!="normal" OR input_voltage < 180
| table _time, pdu_name, rack_id, feed, input_status, input_voltage
```

## Visualization

Status grid (rack × A/B feed status), Table (power events), Timeline (redundancy loss events), Single value (racks with full redundancy %).
